ucs.title: [LLM] Dark Caracal Bandook RAT loader chain — Office/Outlook → mshta/PowerShell → Run-key persistence
ucs.description: Hunts Dark Caracal's signature Bandook RAT staging pattern: a spearphished Office document (T1566.003 / T1204.002) spawns a script-host or HTA loader (T1059.003 / T1218.001) that drops a payload into a user-writable directory and pins it with a HKCU\Run autorun (T1547.001) — all on the same host within a tight window. This is the chain documented by Lookout (2018) and Check Point (Bandook: Signed & Delivered, 2020-2023) for the GDGS-attributed crew tracked as Dark Caracal / Bandook Group.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as loader_time max(_time) as loader_last from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","mshta.exe","hh.exe")) AND (Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","hh.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")) AND NOT (Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as host | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as reg_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where (Registry.registry_path="*\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce\\*") AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*\\AppData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Public\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Temp\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*.dll*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*.hta*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*.vbs*") by Registry.dest Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | rename dest as host ] | eval delta=abs(reg_time-loader_time) | where delta < 1800 | table loader_time reg_time delta host user parent_process_name process_name process registry_path registry_value_name registry_value_data | sort - loader_time
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let CorrelationWindow = 30m;
let _office_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","mshta.exe","hh.exe"]);
let _loaders = dynamic(["mshta.exe","hh.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe"]);
let Loader = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_office_parents)
    | where FileName in~ (_loaders)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project LoaderTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ParentFile = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ChildFile = FileName,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
| where RegistryValueData has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Public\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", ".dll", ".hta", ".vbs", ".js", ".bat")
| join kind=inner Loader on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (LoaderTime .. LoaderTime + CorrelationWindow)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, LoaderTime)
| project LoaderTime, RunKeyTime = Timestamp, DelaySec,
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentFile, ParentCmd, ChildFile, ChildCmd,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData,
          InitiatingProcessFileName
| order by LoaderTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let CorrelationMin = 30;
let _office_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","mshta.exe","hh.exe"]);
let _loaders = dynamic(["mshta.exe","hh.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe"]);
let Loader = ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | extend ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, '\\')[-1])),
             ChildBin  = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, '\\')[-1]))
    | where ParentBin in (_office_parents)
    | where ChildBin in (_loaders)
    | project LoaderTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              ParentProcessName, ParentBin, TargetProcessName, ChildBin,
              TargetProcessCommandLine, ParentProcessCommandLine;
ImRegistryEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventType has_any ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
| where RegistryValueData has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Public\", @"\Temp\", @"\ProgramData\", ".dll", ".hta", ".vbs", ".js", ".bat")
| join kind=inner Loader on $left.DvcHostname == $right.DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (LoaderTime .. LoaderTime + CorrelationMin * 1m)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, LoaderTime)
| project LoaderTime, RunKeyTime = TimeGenerated, DelaySec,
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ParentBin, ChildBin, TargetProcessCommandLine,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData
| order by LoaderTime desc
ucs.rationale: Dark Caracal's Bandook tradecraft is a 3-event signature: (1) Office/Outlook spawns a script proxy after a Facebook/WhatsApp-delivered lure (T1566.003 is uniquely listed for them), (2) the loader stages a packed binary into AppData/ProgramData, (3) HKCU\Run is set to that path within seconds. Generic 'Office spawns PowerShell' rules fire on every macro; this UC requires the Run-key drop on the SAM

ucs.title: [LLM] Dark Caracal CHM (hh.exe) execution from user-writable path or remote URL — T1218.001
ucs.description: Hunts the compiled-HTML-help abuse pattern Dark Caracal uses for both initial execution and second-stage delivery: hh.exe loading a .chm file from %TEMP%/AppData/Downloads or directly from an HTTP(S) URL. T1218.001 is explicitly on Dark Caracal's MITRE technique list and is otherwise rarely abused by commodity crews — making this a high-signal hunt for this specific actor.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_hash) as sha256 from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="hh.exe" AND NOT (Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE")) by Processes.dest Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | search (cmdline="*\\AppData\\*" OR cmdline="*\\Temp\\*" OR cmdline="*\\Downloads\\*" OR cmdline="*\\Public\\*" OR cmdline="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR cmdline="*http://*" OR cmdline="*https://*" OR cmdline="*\\\\*\\*") | eval suspect_chm=if(match(cmdline,"(?i)\\.chm"),1,0) | where suspect_chm=1 | rename dest as host | table firstTime host user parent cmdline sha256 | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName =~ "hh.exe"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where ProcessCommandLine has ".chm"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", "http://", "https://", @"\\")
// Suppress legit help-file viewing from Program Files / Windows
| where not (ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"C:\Program Files\", @"C:\Program Files (x86)\", @"C:\Windows\Help\"))
| extend ChmPath = extract(@"(?i)([a-z]:\\[^\"\s]+\.chm|https?://[^\"\s]+\.chm|\\\\[^\"\s]+\.chm)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ChmPath,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, '\\')[-1]))
| where ChildBin == "hh.exe"
| where TargetProcessCommandLine has ".chm"
| where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Temp\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", "http://", "https://", @"\\")
| where not (TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (@"C:\Program Files\", @"C:\Program Files (x86)\", @"C:\Windows\Help\"))
| extend ChmPath = extract(@"(?i)([a-z]:\\[^\"\s]+\.chm|https?://[^\"\s]+\.chm|\\\\[^\"\s]+\.chm)", 1, TargetProcessCommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ChmPath,
          TargetProcessCommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, ParentProcessCommandLine, SHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Dark Caracal CHM Proxy Execution from User-Writable Path
id: a4f1b2c3-d5e6-47f8-9012-dcc1c0d4ec70
status: experimental
description: Detects hh.exe loading a .chm file from a user-writable directory or remote URL — T1218.001 abuse pattern associated with Dark Caracal (G0070) / Bandook Group operators.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0070/
  - https://info.lookout.com/rs/051-ESQ-475/images/Lookout_Dark-Caracal_srr_20180118_us_v.1.0.pdf
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026-05-03
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1218.001
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.g0070
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\hh.exe'
  selection_chm:
    CommandLine|contains: '.chm'
  selection_path:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\Temp\'
      - '\Downloads\'
      - '\Public\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - 'http://'
      - 'https://'
      - '\\'
  filter_legit:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
      - 'C:\Windows\Help\'
  condition: selection_img and selection_chm and selection_path and not filter_legit
falsepositives:
  - Custom in-house help packages stored under user profiles
level: high
ucs.rationale: T1218.001 (CHM via hh.exe) is one of only ~12 ATT&CK techniques explicitly tracked for Dark Caracal — most adversaries don't touch CHM at all. Legit hh.exe almost always loads .chm files from C:\Program Files\* or C:\Windows\Help\*; loading from %AppData%, %Temp%, Downloads, a UNC share, or directly from HTTP is overwhelmingly malicious and matches Lookout's documented Dark Caracal/Bandook deliver

_field_issues.message: `Program` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Files` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `FileSize`?
`Windows` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`Help` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ChmPath` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `Program` is not a canonical column of ImProcessCreate.
`Files` is not a canonical column of ImProcessCreate.
`Windows` is not a canonical column of ImProcessCreate.
`Help` is not a canonical column of ImProcessCreate.
`ChmPath` is not a canonical column of ImProcessCreate.
